refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize energy consumption per ton moved to meet or exceed the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the high-level FR into a measurable and actionable sub-FR focused on energy consumption per ton moved aligns with the acceptance criteria and enables targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure energy used and tons moved over the evaluation period and calculate energy_per_ton to verify it meets ≤ E_target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor and report energy consumption accurately.
FR2: Optimize vehicle operation to reduce energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Provide feedback and control to maintain energy efficiency targets.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement real-time energy and load telemetry sensors and data aggregation.
DP2: Develop energy-efficient vehicle control algorithms optimizing powertrain and load handling.
DP3: Integrate energy efficiency monitoring and alerting system for operator feedback.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement real-time energy and load telemetry sensors and data aggregation.

potential_sources: Telemetry data dependency between energy monitoring and vehicle control.
Feedback loop coupling between monitoring and operator alerting.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically precedes DP2 and DP3, enabling modular design with manageable coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate and reliable sensor data can be obtained in harsh worksite conditions.
Risk of sensor failure impacting energy efficiency measurement accuracy.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing sensor data acquisition separately from control algorithms to reduce coupling.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical components including sensors and embedded control units responsible for energy measurement and control.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends real-time energy consumption and load data to NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational commands and optimization parameters from NGHE Solution.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as sensors and control logic are physically embedded; low diffusion risk.
Receives telemetry data but does not contain sensor hardware; moderate diffusion risk.
Uses telemetry data for remote control but does not own energy measurement; higher diffusion risk.
Minimal involvement in energy measurement; high diffusion risk if included.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy measurement and control logic are best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize interface complexity and ensure data integrity.
containment_risks: Potential hardware failure in Vehicle sensors impacting system-wide energy efficiency monitoring.
Limited direct control from NGHE Solution may delay system-level optimization feedback.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate adding a dedicated energy management submodule within Vehicle to isolate sensor and control functions.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (real-time energy telemetry) directly addresses the refined FR and supports measurable verification.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry data shared with multiple modules may increase interface complexity.
Potential duplication of energy data processing if not centralized.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Assigning DP to Vehicle module ensures physical containment of sensors and reduces diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Energy consumption data can be verified via telemetry and physical measurements within Vehicle module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear ownership of energy measurement within Vehicle module aligns with physical realization.

recommended_revisions: Consider modularizing energy data processing to avoid duplication across NGHE Solution and Teleoperation.
Assess robustness of sensor hardware under safety-critical conditions.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, risks related to sensor reliability and data diffusion warrant refinement before proceeding.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor and report energy consumption accurately.
FR2: Optimize vehicle operation to reduce energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Provide feedback and control to maintain energy efficiency targets.

revise.why: To modularize energy data acquisition and processing to reduce coupling and diffusion risks.

assumptions: Reliable sensor data acquisition is feasible in operational environment.
Vehicle module can host necessary hardware and embedded software.
open_risks: Sensor hardware failure impacting energy efficiency measurement.
Data diffusion causing inconsistent energy efficiency reporting.
evidence_needed: Sensor performance data under worksite conditions.
Interface specifications for telemetry data sharing.
